items.title: Het Nederlandse tintje bij feniksclub en cupfighter Macclesfield
items.summary: Honderdzeventien. Dat is, gemeten in het aantal plekken in de voetbalpiramide, de afstand tussen Macclesfield FC en Crystal Palace. Macclesfield, de in 2020 heropgerichte subtopper in de National League North, zorgde voor een historische sensatie door op het eigen Moss Rose met 2-1 van de bekerwinnaar te winnen en speelt maandagavond in de vierde ronde van de FA Cup tegen Brentford. Eerder verscheen op VI PRO al het verhaal van de club , nu ruim baan voor de mensen die Macclesfield Town vormen. Met een Nederlands tintje!

items.title: FA Cup news and buildup; Wrexham joy; Guimarães ruled out for eight weeks – matchday live
items.summary: Buildup to the weekend’s football action Things to look out for | Follow us | Mail us here From Arsenal’s midfield conundrum to Newcastle’s misfiring attack, here are 10 things to look out for in the FA Cup this weekend. Paris St Germain’s Ligue 1 title hopes suffered a blow with a 3-1 defeat by managerless Rennes at Roazhon Park. Luis Enrique’s side moved back into top spot ahead of Lens last weekend with a 5-0 hammering of Marseille, their seventh success in a row in the league, but they were swiftly brought back down to earth in Brittany. Continue reading...

items.title: Transfers Ajax: target JP Chermont alsnog op weg naar uitgang bij Santos
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Ajax op de voet, ook nu de winterse transferwindow gesloten is. De doelwitten van de Amsterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Journalist verrast Guardiola met kennis: 'Wil je mijn assistent worden?'
items.summary: Pep Guardiola toonde zich in de aanloop naar het FA Cup-duel met Salford City onder de indruk van de tactische kennis van een verslaggever.

items.title: Guimaraes set to be out for two months with injury
items.summary: Newcastle United captain Bruno Guimaraes is expected to be out for a couple of months after sustaining a hamstring injury.

items.title: Wrexham into FA Cup last 16 for 1st time since '97
items.summary: Wrexham achieved another landmark moment in their meteoric rise after reaching the last 16 of England's FA Cup for the first time since 1997.

items.title: PSG stunned by coachless Rennes in title blow
items.summary: Coachless Rennes stunned Ligue 1 leaders Paris Saint-Germain 3-1 at home and snapped a five-game winless run on Friday.

items.title: Pulisic comes off bench as Modric gives Milan win
items.summary: Luka Modric scored a late winner for AC Milan to beat Pisa 2-1 and close the gap on Serie A leader Inter Milan to five points on Friday.

items.title: Hull confirm arrests over chanting vs. Chelsea
items.summary: Hull City have confirmed there were multiple arrests made for discriminatory chanting during Friday's 4-0 FA Cup fourth round defeat to Chelsea.

items.title: UEFA wil met VAR terug naar de basis
items.summary: Scheidsrechtersbaas Roberto Rosetti van de UEFA vindt dat de VAR in de loop der jaren te machtig is geworden. De Italiaan wil met de video-arbitrage terug naar de basis.

items.title: AC Milan geniet van Modric: 'Kunnen allemaal iets leren van hem'
items.summary: AC Milan verloor nog maar één keer dit seizoen en ontpopt zich in de Serie A als voornaamste uitdager van Internazionale. Vrijdagavond ontsnapte de nummer twee van de Italiaanse competitie, met dank aan good old Luka Modric. AC Milan-coach Massimiliano Allegri is om meerdere redenen blij met Modric.

items.title: Scorecard: England vs Scotland, T20 World Cup
Ireland stay alive at T20 World Cup after smashing highest score yet
items.summary: Ireland thumped the highest score of the 2026 Men's T20 World Cup so far as they kept their hopes of reaching the Super 8s alive with a 96-run win over Oman in Colombo.

items.title: Why Villa are still relying on old guard - and can it last?
items.summary: Aston Villa's old guard have taken the club to new heights - but how long can it last?

items.title: Leicester and Liverpool locked in tense race to avoid WSL relegation playoff
items.summary: League expansion offers top-flight teams a greater chance of staying up but jeopardy remains in contest to steer clear of finishing bottom The state of play at the top of Women’s Super League, with Manchester City 11 points clear of second-placed Manchester United, means jeopardy has to be found elsewhere. There is excitement to be had in the likely scenario of a different name on the WSL trophy for the first time in seven years, but at this point we are watching a procession rather than a battle, despite Arsenal’s impressive 1-0 win over City last weekend. Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham job has become a public meat grinder and the fans’ pain is more content | Barney Ronay
items.summary: Ritualistic Spurs manager sacking is a marker in the year, but the failing lies with executives responsible for some really vague recruitment Don’t talk about Spurs. Don’t talk about Spurs. Don’t keep returning to Spurs, bloodshot and shivering. Don’t end up twitching on a Manhattan street corner, nodding at Jean-Michel Basquiat as he drifts past, waiting for your Spurs man to appear out of a fire escape, uncork his Spurs pouch, and say what do you need, while you chatter about just wanting to return to the club DNA, whatever that is, nobody knows, but it’s Spurs, and Spurs is your wife and it’s your life and, you know, sources close to sources say a swoop for German wunder-coach Helmut von Wangerburg may actually be at an advanced … So, Spurs then. It’s true that the media are addicted to this club. But it is also an understandable response to an entity that has become a content machine, perfectly structured to meet the requirements of any moreishly successful streaming drama. Continue reading...

items.title: Scotland's Carrillo claims world title in dramatic win
items.summary: Nico Carrillo finally has a world championship belt around his waist.

items.title: McIlroy six off leaders at Pebble Beach but sees weather hope
items.summary: Rory McIlroy insists he is still in contention after reaching the halfway mark of the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am six shots off the lead.
